database.c(3) Library Functions Manual database.c(3)

NAME

database.c

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define SELECT_SESSION_BASE

Functions


asciichat_error_t database_init (const char *db_path, sqlite3 **db)
Initialize database and create schema. void database_close (sqlite3 *db)
Close database. asciichat_error_t database_session_create (sqlite3 *db, const acip_session_create_t *req, const acds_config_t *config, acip_session_created_t *resp)
Create new session. asciichat_error_t database_session_lookup (sqlite3 *db, const char *session_string, const acds_config_t *config, acip_session_info_t *resp)
Lookup session by string. asciichat_error_t database_session_join (sqlite3 *db, const acip_session_join_t *req, const acds_config_t *config, acip_session_joined_t *resp)
Join existing session. asciichat_error_t database_session_leave (sqlite3 *db, const uint8_t session_id[16], const uint8_t participant_id[16])
Leave session. session_entry_t * database_session_find_by_id (sqlite3 *db, const uint8_t session_id[16])
Find session by session_id. session_entry_t * database_session_find_by_string (sqlite3 *db, const char *session_string)
Find session by session_string. void database_session_cleanup_expired (sqlite3 *db)
Clean up inactive sessions. asciichat_error_t database_session_update_host (sqlite3 *db, const uint8_t session_id[16], const uint8_t host_participant_id[16], const char *host_address, uint16_t host_port, uint8_t connection_type)
Update session host (discovery mode). asciichat_error_t database_session_clear_host (sqlite3 *db, const uint8_t session_id[16])
Clear session host (discovery mode - host migration). asciichat_error_t database_session_start_migration (sqlite3 *db, const uint8_t session_id[16])
Start host migration (discovery mode). bool database_session_is_migration_ready (sqlite3 *db, const uint8_t session_id[16], uint64_t migration_window_ms)
Check if migration window has completed. asciichat_error_t database_session_add_key (sqlite3 *db, const char *session_string, const uint8_t identity_pubkey[32], uint32_t key_version)
Add a key to a session. asciichat_error_t database_session_revoke_key (sqlite3 *db, const char *session_string, const uint8_t identity_pubkey[32])
Revoke a key from a session. bool database_session_verify_key (sqlite3 *db, const char *session_string, const uint8_t identity_pubkey[32])
Check if a key is valid for a session (exists and not revoked). asciichat_error_t database_session_get_keys (sqlite3 *db, const char *session_string, uint8_t(*keys_out)[32], size_t max_keys, size_t *count_out)
Get all active keys for a session. asciichat_error_t database_stats_session_count (sqlite3 *db, int64_t *count_out)
Get total number of active sessions. asciichat_error_t database_stats_unique_clients (sqlite3 *db, int64_t *count_out)
Get total number of unique clients/participants.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define SELECT_SESSION_BASE

Value:

"SELECT session_string, session_id, host_pubkey, password_hash, "                                                    \
"max_participants, current_participants, capabilities, has_password, "                                               \
"expose_ip_publicly, session_type, server_address, server_port, "                                                    \
"created_at, expires_at, last_activity_at, initiator_id, host_established, "                                         \
"host_participant_id, host_address, host_port, host_connection_type, "                                               \
"in_migration, migration_start_ns FROM sessions"

  discovery/database.c.

26   "SELECT session_string, session_id, host_pubkey, password_hash, "                                                    \
27   "max_participants, current_participants, capabilities, has_password, "                                               \
28   "expose_ip_publicly, session_type, server_address, server_port, "                                                    \
29   "created_at, expires_at, last_activity_at, initiator_id, host_established, "                                         \
30   "host_participant_id, host_address, host_port, host_connection_type, "                                               \
31   "in_migration, migration_start_ns FROM sessions"

Function
  Documentation




void
  database_close (sqlite3 * db)


Close database.


Parameters


db Database handle to close


Definition at line 310 of file
  discovery/database.c.


310                                  {
311   if (!db) {
312     return;
313   }
314   sqlite3_close(db);
315   log_debug("Database closed");
316 }






References log_debug.


Referenced by acds_server_init(), and
    acds_server_shutdown().






asciichat_error_t database_init (const char * db_path,
  sqlite3 ** db)


Initialize database and create schema.


Parameters


db_path Path to SQLite database file


db Output database handle


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 263 of file
  discovery/database.c.


263                                                                    {
264   if (!db_path || !db) {
265     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db_path or db is NULL");
266   }
267 
268   log_info("Opening database: %s", db_path);
269 
270   int rc = sqlite3_open(db_path, db);
271   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
272     const char *err = sqlite3_errmsg(*db);
273     sqlite3_close(*db);
274     *db = NULL;
275     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to open database: %s", err);
276   }
277 
278   // Enable Write-Ahead Logging for concurrent reads
279   char *err_msg = NULL;
280   rc = sqlite3_exec(*db, "PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;", NULL, NULL, &err_msg);
281   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
282     log_warn("Failed to enable WAL mode: %s", err_msg ? err_msg : "unknown error");
283     sqlite3_free(err_msg);
284   }
285 
286   // Enable foreign key constraints
287   rc = sqlite3_exec(*db, "PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON;", NULL, NULL, &err_msg);
288   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
289     log_error("Failed to enable foreign keys: %s", err_msg ? err_msg : "unknown error");
290     sqlite3_free(err_msg);
291     sqlite3_close(*db);
292     *db = NULL;
293     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to enable foreign keys");
294   }
295 
296   // Create schema
297   rc = sqlite3_exec(*db, schema_sql, NULL, NULL, &err_msg);
298   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
299     log_error("Failed to create schema: %s", err_msg ? err_msg : "unknown error");
300     sqlite3_free(err_msg);
301     sqlite3_close(*db);
302     *db = NULL;
303     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to create database schema");
304   }
305 
306   log_info("Database initialized successfully (SQLite as single source of truth)");
307   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
308 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_error, log_info,
    log_warn, and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by acds_server_init().






asciichat_error_t database_session_add_key (sqlite3 * db,
  const char * session_string, const uint8_t identity_pubkey[32],
  uint32_t key_version)


Add a key to a session.


Parameters


db Database handle


session_string Session string


identity_pubkey Ed25519 public key (32 bytes)


key_version Key version for tracking rotation


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 1049 of file
    discovery/database.c.


1050                                                                  {
1051   if (!db || !session_string || !identity_pubkey) {
1052     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for add_key");
1053   }
1054 
1055   const char *sql = "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO session_keys (session_string, identity_pubkey, key_version, added_at) "
1056                     "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)";
1057 
1058   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
1059   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) != SQLITE_OK) {
1060     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare add_key statement: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1061   }
1062 
1063   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
1064   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 2, identity_pubkey, 32, SQLITE_STATIC);
1065   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 3, key_version);
1066   sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 4, database_get_current_time_ms());
1067 
1068   int rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
1069   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1070 
1071   if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
1072     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to add session key: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1073   }
1074 
1075   log_debug("Added key to session %s (version=%u)", session_string, key_version);
1076   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1077 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug, and SET_ERRNO.






void
  database_session_cleanup_expired (sqlite3 * db)


Clean up inactive sessions. Removes sessions that have been
    inactive for more than 3 hours. Inactivity is measured by last_activity_at
    (updated on join/leave/host updates). Called periodically by background
    cleanup thread.




Parameters


db Database handle


Definition at line 844 of file
  discovery/database.c.


844                                                    {
845   if (!db) {
846     return;
847   }
848 
849   uint64_t now = database_get_current_time_ms();
850   // 3 hours in milliseconds
851   uint64_t inactivity_threshold = 3ULL * SEC_PER_HOUR * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
852   uint64_t cutoff_time = now - inactivity_threshold;
853 
854   // Log sessions about to be deleted
855   const char *log_sql = "SELECT session_string, last_activity_at FROM sessions WHERE last_activity_at < ?";
856   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
857   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, log_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) == SQLITE_OK) {
858     sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 1, (sqlite3_int64)cutoff_time);
859     while (sqlite3_step(stmt) == SQLITE_ROW) {
860       const char *session_string = (const char *)sqlite3_column_text(stmt, 0);
861       uint64_t last_activity = (uint64_t)sqlite3_column_int64(stmt, 1);
862       uint64_t inactive_ms = now - last_activity;
863       uint64_t inactive_hours = inactive_ms / (SEC_PER_HOUR * MS_PER_SEC_INT);
864       log_info("Session %s inactive for %lu hours, deleting", session_string ? session_string : "<unknown>",
865                inactive_hours);
866     }
867     sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
868   }
869 
870   // Delete inactive sessions (CASCADE deletes participants)
871   const char *del_sql = "DELETE FROM sessions WHERE last_activity_at < ?";
872   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, del_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) == SQLITE_OK) {
873     sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 1, (sqlite3_int64)cutoff_time);
874     sqlite3_step(stmt);
875     int deleted = sqlite3_changes(db);
876     sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
877 
878     if (deleted > 0) {
879       log_info("Cleaned up %d inactive sessions (>3 hours)", deleted);
880     }
881   }
882 }






References log_info, MS_PER_SEC_INT, and
    SEC_PER_HOUR.






asciichat_error_t database_session_clear_host (sqlite3 *
  db, const uint8_t session_id[16])


Clear session host (discovery mode - host migration). Called when
    the current host disconnects or fails. Clears host fields so remaining
    participants can negotiate a new host.




Parameters


db Database handle


session_id Session UUID


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 933 of file
  discovery/database.c.


933                                                                                          {
934   if (!db || !session_id) {
935     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db or session_id is NULL");
936   }
937 
938   const char *sql = "UPDATE sessions SET "
939                     "host_established = 0, "
940                     "host_participant_id = NULL, "
941                     "host_address = NULL, "
942                     "host_port = 0, "
943                     "host_connection_type = 0 "
944                     "WHERE session_id = ?";
945 
946   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
947   int rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
948   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
949     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare host clear: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
950   }
951 
952   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 1, session_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
953 
954   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
955   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
956 
957   if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
958     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to clear host: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
959   }
960 
961   int changes = sqlite3_changes(db);
962   if (changes == 0) {
963     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Session not found for host clear");
964   }
965 
966   log_info("Session host cleared (session=%02x%02x...) - ready for migration", session_id[0], session_id[1]);
967 
968   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
969 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, log_info,
    session_id, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t database_session_create (sqlite3 * db,
  const acip_session_create_t * req, const acds_config_t * config,
  acip_session_created_t * resp)


Create new session.


Parameters


db Database handle


req Session creation request


Config Module ACDS server configuration


resp Session creation response (output)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 322 of file
  discovery/database.c.


323                                                                         {
324   if (!db || !req || !config || !resp) {
325     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db, req, config, or resp is NULL");
326   }
327 
328   memset(resp, 0, sizeof(*resp));
329 
330   // Generate or use reserved session string
331   char session_string[ACIP_MAX_SESSION_STRING_LEN] = {0};
332   if (req->reserved_string_len > 0) {
333     const char *reserved_str = (const char *)(req + 1);
334     size_t len = req->reserved_string_len < (ACIP_MAX_SESSION_STRING_LEN - 1) ? req->reserved_string_len
335                                                                               : (ACIP_MAX_SESSION_STRING_LEN - 1);
336     memcpy(session_string, reserved_str, len);
337     session_string[len] = '\0';
338 
339     if (!is_session_string(session_string)) {
340       asciichat_error_context_t ctx;
341       if (HAS_ERRNO(&ctx)) {
342         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid session string: %s (%s)", session_string, ctx.context_message);
343       }
344       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid session string: %s", session_string);
345     }
346   } else {
347     asciichat_error_t result = acds_string_generate(session_string, sizeof(session_string));
348     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
349       return result;
350     }
351   }
352 
353   // Generate session ID
354   uint8_t session_id[16];
355   generate_uuid(session_id);
356 
357   // Set timestamps
358   uint64_t now = database_get_current_time_ms();
359   uint64_t expires_at = now + ACIP_SESSION_EXPIRATION_MS;
360 
361   // Calculate max_participants
362   uint8_t max_participants =
363       req->max_participants > 0 && req->max_participants <= MAX_PARTICIPANTS ? req->max_participants : MAX_PARTICIPANTS;
364 
365   // Insert into database
366   const char *sql = "INSERT INTO sessions "
367                     "(session_string, session_id, host_pubkey, password_hash, max_participants, "
368                     "current_participants, capabilities, has_password, expose_ip_publicly, "
369                     "session_type, server_address, server_port, created_at, expires_at, last_activity_at) "
370                     "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 0, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)";
371 
372   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
373   int rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
374   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
375     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare session insert: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
376   }
377 
378   log_info("DATABASE_SESSION_CREATE: expose_ip_publicly=%d, server_address='%s' server_port=%u, session_type=%u, "
379            "has_password=%u",
380            req->expose_ip_publicly, req->server_address, req->server_port, req->session_type, req->has_password);
381   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
382   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 2, session_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
383   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 3, req->identity_pubkey, 32, SQLITE_STATIC);
384   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 4, req->has_password ? (const char *)req->password_hash : NULL, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
385   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 5, max_participants);
386   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 6, req->capabilities);
387   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 7, req->has_password ? 1 : 0);
388   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 8, req->expose_ip_publicly ? 1 : 0);
389   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 9, req->session_type);
390   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 10, req->server_address, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
391   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 11, req->server_port);
392   sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 12, (sqlite3_int64)now);
393   sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 13, (sqlite3_int64)expires_at);
394   sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 14, (sqlite3_int64)now); // last_activity_at = created_at
395 
396   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
397   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
398 
399   if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
400     if (rc == SQLITE_CONSTRAINT) {
401       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Session string already exists: %s", session_string);
402     }
403     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to insert session: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
404   }
405 
406   // Fill response
407   resp->session_string_len = (uint8_t)strlen(session_string);
408   SAFE_STRNCPY(resp->session_string, session_string, sizeof(resp->session_string));
409   memcpy(resp->session_id, session_id, 16);
410   resp->expires_at = expires_at;
411   resp->stun_count = config->stun_count;
412   resp->turn_count = config->turn_count;
413 
414   log_info("Session created: %s (session_id=%02x%02x%02x%02x..., max_participants=%d, has_password=%d)", session_string,
415            session_id[0], session_id[1], session_id[2], session_id[3], max_participants, req->has_password ? 1 : 0);
416 
417   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
418 }






References acds_string_generate(),
    ACIP_MAX_SESSION_STRING_LEN, acip_session_create_t,
    acip_session_created_t, ACIP_SESSION_EXPIRATION_MS,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, asciichat_error_context_t::context_message,
    ERROR_CONFIG, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    HAS_ERRNO, is_session_string(), log_info,
    MAX_PARTICIPANTS, SAFE_STRNCPY, session_id,
    SET_ERRNO, acds_config_t::stun_count, and
    acds_config_t::turn_count.






session_entry_t * database_session_find_by_id (sqlite3 *
  db, const uint8_t session_id[16])


Find session by session_id. Allocates and returns a
    session_entry_t. Caller must free with
    session_entry_destroy().




Parameters


db Database handle


session_id Session UUID to find


Returns


Allocated session entry (caller owns), or NULL if not
  found


Definition at line 792 of file
  discovery/database.c.


792                                                                                         {
793   if (!db || !session_id) {
794     return NULL;
795   }
796 
797   static const char sql[] = SELECT_SESSION_BASE " WHERE session_id = ?";
798 
799   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
800   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) != SQLITE_OK) {
801     return NULL;
802   }
803 
804   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 1, session_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
805 
806   session_entry_t *session = NULL;
807   if (sqlite3_step(stmt) == SQLITE_ROW) {
808     session = load_session_from_row(stmt);
809     if (session) {
810       load_session_participants(db, session);
811     }
812   }
813 
814   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
815   return session;
816 }






References SELECT_SESSION_BASE, and session_id.


Referenced by signaling_broadcast(),
    signaling_relay_ice(), signaling_relay_network_quality(), and
    signaling_relay_sdp().

Find session by session_string. Allocates and returns a
    session_entry_t. Caller must free with
    session_entry_destroy().

db Database handle


session_string Session string to find


Returns


Allocated session entry (caller owns), or NULL if not
  found

  discovery/database.c.

820     return NULL;
821   }
822 
823   static const char sql[] = SELECT_SESSION_BASE " WHERE session_string = ?";
824

826   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) != SQLITE_OK) {
827     return NULL;
828   }
829 
830   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
831 
832   session_entry_t *session = NULL;
833   if (sqlite3_step(stmt) == SQLITE_ROW) {
834     session = load_session_from_row(stmt);
835     if (session) {

837     }

asciichat_error_t database_session_get_keys (sqlite3 * db,
  const char * session_string, uint8_t(*) keys_out[32], size_t max_keys,
  size_t * count_out)


Get all active keys for a session.


Parameters


db Database handle


session_string Session string


keys_out Output array for keys (caller must provide buffer)


max_keys Maximum number of keys to retrieve


count_out Output: number of keys retrieved


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 1127 of file
    discovery/database.c.


1128                                                                                 {
1129   if (!db || !session_string || !keys_out || !count_out) {
1130     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for get_keys");
1131   }
1132 
1133   const char *sql = "SELECT identity_pubkey FROM session_keys WHERE session_string = ? AND revoked = 0 "
1134                     "ORDER BY key_version ASC";
1135 
1136   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
1137   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) != SQLITE_OK) {
1138     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare get_keys statement: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1139   }
1140 
1141   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
1142 
1143   size_t count = 0;
1144   while (sqlite3_step(stmt) == SQLITE_ROW && count < max_keys) {
1145     const void *blob = sqlite3_column_blob(stmt, 0);
1146     if (blob && sqlite3_column_bytes(stmt, 0) == 32) {
1147       memcpy(keys_out[count], blob, 32);
1148       count++;
1149     }
1150   }
1151 
1152   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1153   *count_out = count;
1154 
1155   log_debug("Retrieved %zu keys for session %s", count, session_string);
1156   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1157 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug, and SET_ERRNO.






bool database_session_is_migration_ready (sqlite3 * db,
  const uint8_t session_id[16], uint64_t migration_window_ms)


Check if migration window has completed. Called periodically to
    check if migration collection window timeout has expired. Returns the
    session if migration is ready for host election.




Parameters


db Database handle


session_id Session UUID


migration_window_ms Collection window duration in milliseconds


Returns


true if collection window has completed, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 1010 of file
    discovery/database.c.


1010                                                                                                                   {
1011   if (!db || !session_id) {
1012     return false;
1013   }
1014 
1015   uint64_t now_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
1016   const char *sql = "SELECT in_migration, migration_start_ns FROM sessions WHERE session_id = ?";
1017 
1018   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
1019   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) != SQLITE_OK) {
1020     return false;
1021   }
1022 
1023   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 1, session_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
1024 
1025   bool ready = false;
1026   if (sqlite3_step(stmt) == SQLITE_ROW) {
1027     int in_migration = sqlite3_column_int(stmt, 0);
1028     uint64_t migration_start_ns = (uint64_t)sqlite3_column_int64(stmt, 1);
1029 
1030     if (in_migration) {
1031       uint64_t elapsed_ns = now_ns - migration_start_ns;
1032       uint64_t migration_window_ns = migration_window_ms * NS_PER_MS_INT;
1033       if (elapsed_ns >= migration_window_ns) {
1034         ready = true;
1035         log_debug("Migration window complete (session=%02x%02x..., elapsed=%lu ns, window=%lu ns)", session_id[0],
1036                   session_id[1], elapsed_ns, migration_window_ns);
1037       }
1038     }
1039   }
1040 
1041   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1042   return ready;
1043 }






References log_debug, NS_PER_MS_INT,
    session_id, and time_get_realtime_ns().






asciichat_error_t database_session_join (sqlite3 * db,
  const acip_session_join_t * req, const acds_config_t * config,
  acip_session_joined_t * resp)


Join existing session.


Parameters


db Database handle


req Join request


Config Module ACDS server configuration (for TURN
  credentials)


resp Join response (output)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 482 of file
  discovery/database.c.


483                                                                      {
484   if (!db || !req || !config || !resp) {
485     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db, req, config, or resp is NULL");
486   }
487 
488   memset(resp, 0, sizeof(*resp));
489   resp->success = 0;
490 
491   // Extract session string
492   char session_string[ACIP_MAX_SESSION_STRING_LEN] = {0};
493   size_t len = req->session_string_len < (ACIP_MAX_SESSION_STRING_LEN - 1) ? req->session_string_len
494                                                                            : (ACIP_MAX_SESSION_STRING_LEN - 1);
495   memcpy(session_string, req->session_string, len);
496   session_string[len] = '\0';
497 
498   // Find session
499   session_entry_t *session = database_session_find_by_string(db, session_string);
500   if (!session) {
501     resp->error_code = ACIP_ERROR_SESSION_NOT_FOUND;
502     SAFE_STRNCPY(resp->error_message, "Session not found", sizeof(resp->error_message));
503     log_warn("Session join failed: %s (not found)", session_string);
504     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
505   }
506 
507   // Check if session is full
508   if (session->current_participants >= session->max_participants) {
509     session_entry_destroy(session);
510     resp->error_code = ACIP_ERROR_SESSION_FULL;
511     SAFE_STRNCPY(resp->error_message, "Session is full", sizeof(resp->error_message));
512     log_warn("Session join failed: %s (full)", session_string);
513     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
514   }
515 
516   // Verify password if required
517   if (session->has_password && req->has_password) {
518     if (!verify_password(req->password, session->password_hash)) {
519       session_entry_destroy(session);
520       resp->error_code = ACIP_ERROR_INVALID_PASSWORD;
521       SAFE_STRNCPY(resp->error_message, "Invalid password", sizeof(resp->error_message));
522       log_warn("Session join failed: %s (invalid password)", session_string);
523       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
524     }
525   } else if (session->has_password && !req->has_password) {
526     session_entry_destroy(session);
527     resp->error_code = ACIP_ERROR_INVALID_PASSWORD;
528     SAFE_STRNCPY(resp->error_message, "Password required", sizeof(resp->error_message));
529     log_warn("Session join failed: %s (password required)", session_string);
530     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
531   }
532 
533   // Generate participant ID
534   uint8_t participant_id[16];
535   generate_uuid(participant_id);
536   uint64_t now = database_get_current_time_ms();
537 
538   // Begin transaction
539   char *err_msg = NULL;
540   int rc = sqlite3_exec(db, "BEGIN IMMEDIATE;", NULL, NULL, &err_msg);
541   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
542     session_entry_destroy(session);
543     log_error("Failed to begin transaction: %s", err_msg ? err_msg : "unknown");
544     sqlite3_free(err_msg);
545     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to begin transaction");
546   }
547 
548   // Insert participant
549   const char *insert_sql = "INSERT INTO participants (participant_id, session_string, identity_pubkey, joined_at) "
550                            "VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)";
551   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
552   rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, insert_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
553   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
554     sqlite3_exec(db, "ROLLBACK;", NULL, NULL, NULL);
555     session_entry_destroy(session);
556     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare participant insert: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
557   }
558 
559   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 1, participant_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
560   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 2, session->session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
561   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 3, req->identity_pubkey, 32, SQLITE_STATIC);
562   sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 4, (sqlite3_int64)now);
563 
564   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
565   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
566 
567   if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
568     sqlite3_exec(db, "ROLLBACK;", NULL, NULL, NULL);
569     session_entry_destroy(session);
570     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to insert participant: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
571   }
572 
573   // Update participant count and last activity
574   const char *update_sql = "UPDATE sessions SET current_participants = current_participants + 1, "
575                            "last_activity_at = ? WHERE session_string = ?";
576   rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, update_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
577   if (rc == SQLITE_OK) {
578     sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 1, (sqlite3_int64)now);
579     sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 2, session->session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
580     sqlite3_step(stmt);
581     sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
582   }
583 
584   // Set initiator_id if this is the first participant (for discovery mode tiebreaker)
585   bool is_first_participant = (session->current_participants == 0);
586   bool is_zero_initiator = true;
587   for (int i = 0; i < 16; i++) {
588     if (session->initiator_id[i] != 0) {
589       is_zero_initiator = false;
590       break;
591     }
592   }
593 
594   if (is_first_participant || is_zero_initiator) {
595     const char *initiator_sql = "UPDATE sessions SET initiator_id = ? WHERE session_string = ?";
596     rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, initiator_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
597     if (rc == SQLITE_OK) {
598       sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 1, participant_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
599       sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 2, session->session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
600       sqlite3_step(stmt);
601       sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
602       memcpy(session->initiator_id, participant_id, 16);
603       log_debug("Set initiator_id to new participant %02x%02x...", participant_id[0], participant_id[1]);
604     }
605   }
606 
607   // Commit transaction
608   rc = sqlite3_exec(db, "COMMIT;", NULL, NULL, &err_msg);
609   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
610     log_error("Failed to commit transaction: %s", err_msg ? err_msg : "unknown");
611     sqlite3_free(err_msg);
612     sqlite3_exec(db, "ROLLBACK;", NULL, NULL, NULL);
613     session_entry_destroy(session);
614     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to commit transaction");
615   }
616 
617   // Fill response
618   resp->success = 1;
619   resp->error_code = ACIP_ERROR_NONE;
620   memcpy(resp->participant_id, participant_id, 16);
621   memcpy(resp->session_id, session->session_id, 16);
622 
623   // Discovery mode host negotiation fields
624   memcpy(resp->initiator_id, session->initiator_id, 16);
625   resp->host_established = session->host_established ? 1 : 0;
626   if (session->host_established) {
627     memcpy(resp->host_id, session->host_participant_id, 16);
628   }
629   // peer_count = current_participants (excluding self) that need to negotiate
630   resp->peer_count = session->current_participants; // Will be incremented after this returns
631 
632   log_debug("SESSION_JOIN response: session_id=%02x%02x%02x%02x..., participant_id=%02x%02x%02x%02x..., "
633             "initiator=%02x%02x..., host_established=%d, peer_count=%d",
634             resp->session_id[0], resp->session_id[1], resp->session_id[2], resp->session_id[3], resp->participant_id[0],
635             resp->participant_id[1], resp->participant_id[2], resp->participant_id[3], resp->initiator_id[0],
636             resp->initiator_id[1], resp->host_established, resp->peer_count);
637 
638   // IP disclosure logic
639   log_info("DATABASE_SESSION_JOIN: has_password=%d, expose_ip_publicly=%d, server_address='%s'", session->has_password,
640            session->expose_ip_publicly, session->server_address);
641   bool reveal_ip = session->has_password || session->expose_ip_publicly;
642   log_info("DATABASE_SESSION_JOIN: reveal_ip=%d", reveal_ip);
643 
644   if (reveal_ip) {
645     SAFE_STRNCPY(resp->server_address, session->server_address, sizeof(resp->server_address));
646     resp->server_port = session->server_port;
647     resp->session_type = session->session_type;
648 
649     // Generate TURN credentials for WebRTC sessions
650     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && config->turn_secret[0] != '\0') {
651       turn_credentials_t turn_creds;
652       asciichat_error_t turn_result =
653           turn_generate_credentials(session_string, config->turn_secret, 86400, &turn_creds);
654       if (turn_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
655         SAFE_STRNCPY(resp->turn_username, turn_creds.username, sizeof(resp->turn_username));
656         SAFE_STRNCPY(resp->turn_password, turn_creds.password, sizeof(resp->turn_password));
657         log_debug("Generated TURN credentials for session %s", session_string);
658       }
659     }
660 
661     log_info("Participant joined session %s (participants=%d/%d, server=%s:%d, type=%s)", session_string,
662              session->current_participants + 1, session->max_participants, resp->server_address, resp->server_port,
663              session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC ? "WebRTC" : "DirectTCP");
664   } else {
665     log_info("Participant joined session %s (participants=%d/%d, IP WITHHELD)", session_string,
666              session->current_participants + 1, session->max_participants);
667   }
668 
669   session_entry_destroy(session);
670   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
671 }






References ACIP_ERROR_INVALID_PASSWORD,
    ACIP_ERROR_NONE, ACIP_ERROR_SESSION_FULL,
    ACIP_ERROR_SESSION_NOT_FOUND, ACIP_MAX_SESSION_STRING_LEN,
    acip_session_join_t, acip_session_joined_t,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, session_entry::current_participants,
    database_session_find_by_string(), ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, session_entry::expose_ip_publicly,
    session_entry::has_password, session_entry::host_established,
    session_entry::host_participant_id,
    session_entry::initiator_id, log_debug, log_error,
    log_info, log_warn, session_entry::max_participants,
    participant_id, turn_credentials_t::password,
    session_entry::password_hash, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    session_entry::server_address, session_entry::server_port,
    session_entry_destroy(), session_entry::session_id,
    session_entry::session_string, session_entry::session_type,
    SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC, SET_ERRNO,
    turn_generate_credentials(), acds_config_t::turn_secret, and
    turn_credentials_t::username.






asciichat_error_t database_session_leave (sqlite3 * db,
  const uint8_t session_id[16], const uint8_t
  participant_id[16])


Leave session.


Parameters


db Database handle


session_id Session UUID


participant_id Participant UUID


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 673 of file
  discovery/database.c.


673                                                                                                                       {
674   if (!db || !session_id || !participant_id) {
675     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db, session_id, or participant_id is NULL");
676   }
677 
678   // Look up session_string from session_id
679   char session_string[SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE] = {0};
680   const char *lookup_sql = "SELECT session_string FROM sessions WHERE session_id = ?";
681   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
682   int rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, lookup_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
683   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
684     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare session lookup: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
685   }
686 
687   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 1, session_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
688   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
689   if (rc != SQLITE_ROW) {
690     sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
691     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Session not found");
692   }
693 
694   const char *str = (const char *)sqlite3_column_text(stmt, 0);
695   if (str) {
696     SAFE_STRNCPY(session_string, str, sizeof(session_string));
697   }
698   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
699 
700   if (session_string[0] == '\0') {
701     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Session string is empty");
702   }
703 
704   // Begin transaction
705   char *err_msg = NULL;
706   rc = sqlite3_exec(db, "BEGIN IMMEDIATE;", NULL, NULL, &err_msg);
707   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
708     log_error("Failed to begin transaction: %s", err_msg ? err_msg : "unknown");
709     sqlite3_free(err_msg);
710     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to begin transaction");
711   }
712 
713   // Delete participant
714   const char *del_sql = "DELETE FROM participants WHERE participant_id = ? AND session_string = ?";
715   rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, del_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
716   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
717     sqlite3_exec(db, "ROLLBACK;", NULL, NULL, NULL);
718     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare participant delete: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
719   }
720 
721   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 1, participant_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
722   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 2, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
723   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
724   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
725 
726   if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
727     sqlite3_exec(db, "ROLLBACK;", NULL, NULL, NULL);
728     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to delete participant: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
729   }
730 
731   int changes = sqlite3_changes(db);
732   if (changes == 0) {
733     sqlite3_exec(db, "ROLLBACK;", NULL, NULL, NULL);
734     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Participant not in session");
735   }
736 
737   uint64_t now = database_get_current_time_ms();
738 
739   // Decrement participant count and update last activity
740   const char *update_sql = "UPDATE sessions SET current_participants = current_participants - 1, "
741                            "last_activity_at = ? WHERE session_string = ? AND current_participants > 0";
742   rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, update_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
743   if (rc == SQLITE_OK) {
744     sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 1, (sqlite3_int64)now);
745     sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 2, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
746     sqlite3_step(stmt);
747     sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
748   }
749 
750   // Check if session is now empty and delete if so
751   const char *check_sql = "SELECT current_participants FROM sessions WHERE session_string = ?";
752   rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, check_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
753   if (rc == SQLITE_OK) {
754     sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
755     if (sqlite3_step(stmt) == SQLITE_ROW) {
756       int count = sqlite3_column_int(stmt, 0);
757 
758       if (count <= 0) {
759         log_info("Session %s has no participants, deleting", session_string);
760         sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
761 
762         // Delete empty session
763         const char *del_session_sql = "DELETE FROM sessions WHERE session_string = ?";
764         rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, del_session_sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
765         if (rc == SQLITE_OK) {
766           sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
767           sqlite3_step(stmt);
768           sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
769         }
770         stmt = NULL;
771       } else {
772         log_info("Participant left session %s (participants=%d remaining)", session_string, count);
773       }
774     }
775     if (stmt) {
776       sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
777     }
778   }
779 
780   // Commit transaction
781   rc = sqlite3_exec(db, "COMMIT;", NULL, NULL, &err_msg);
782   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
783     log_error("Failed to commit transaction: %s", err_msg ? err_msg : "unknown");
784     sqlite3_free(err_msg);
785     sqlite3_exec(db, "ROLLBACK;", NULL, NULL, NULL);
786     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to commit transaction");
787   }
788 
789   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
790 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, log_error,
    log_info, participant_id, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    session_id, SESSION_STRING_BUFFER_SIZE, and
  SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t database_session_lookup (sqlite3 * db,
  const char * session_string, const acds_config_t * config,
  acip_session_info_t * resp)


Lookup session by string.


Parameters


db Database handle


session_string Session string to look up


Config Module ACDS server configuration (for policy
  flags)


resp Session info response (output)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 420 of file
  discovery/database.c.


421                                                                      {
422   if (!db || !session_string || !config || !resp) {
423     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db, session_string, config, or resp is NULL");
424   }
425 
426   memset(resp, 0, sizeof(*resp));
427 
428   const char *sql = "SELECT session_string, session_id, host_pubkey, password_hash, "
429                     "max_participants, current_participants, capabilities, has_password, "
430                     "expose_ip_publicly, session_type, server_address, server_port, "
431                     "created_at, expires_at, last_activity_at FROM sessions WHERE session_string = ?";
432 
433   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
434   int rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
435   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
436     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare session lookup: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
437   }
438 
439   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
440 
441   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
442   if (rc != SQLITE_ROW) {
443     sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
444     resp->found = 0;
445     log_debug("Session lookup failed: %s (not found)", session_string);
446     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
447   }
448 
449   // Load session data
450   resp->found = 1;
451 
452   const void *blob = sqlite3_column_blob(stmt, 1);
453   if (blob) {
454     memcpy(resp->session_id, blob, 16);
455   }
456 
457   blob = sqlite3_column_blob(stmt, 2);
458   if (blob) {
459     memcpy(resp->host_pubkey, blob, 32);
460   }
461 
462   resp->max_participants = (uint8_t)sqlite3_column_int(stmt, 4);
463   resp->current_participants = (uint8_t)sqlite3_column_int(stmt, 5);
464   resp->capabilities = (uint8_t)sqlite3_column_int(stmt, 6);
465   resp->has_password = sqlite3_column_int(stmt, 7) != 0;
466   resp->session_type = (uint8_t)sqlite3_column_int(stmt, 9);
467   resp->created_at = (uint64_t)sqlite3_column_int64(stmt, 12);
468   resp->expires_at = (uint64_t)sqlite3_column_int64(stmt, 13);
469 
470   // ACDS policy flags
471   resp->require_server_verify = config->require_server_verify ? 1 : 0;
472   resp->require_client_verify = config->require_client_verify ? 1 : 0;
473 
474   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
475 
476   log_debug("Session lookup: %s (found, participants=%d/%d)", session_string, resp->current_participants,
477             resp->max_participants);
478 
479   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
480 }






References acip_session_info_t, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    ERROR_CONFIG, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug,
    acds_config_t::require_client_verify,
    acds_config_t::require_server_verify, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t database_session_revoke_key (sqlite3 *
  db, const char * session_string, const uint8_t
  identity_pubkey[32])


Revoke a key from a session.


Parameters


db Database handle


session_string Session string


identity_pubkey Ed25519 public key to revoke (32 bytes)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 1079 of file
    discovery/database.c.


1080                                                                                  {
1081   if (!db || !session_string || !identity_pubkey) {
1082     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid parameters for revoke_key");
1083   }
1084 
1085   const char *sql = "UPDATE session_keys SET revoked = 1 WHERE session_string = ? AND identity_pubkey = ?";
1086 
1087   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
1088   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) != SQLITE_OK) {
1089     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare revoke_key statement: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1090   }
1091 
1092   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
1093   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 2, identity_pubkey, 32, SQLITE_STATIC);
1094 
1095   int rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
1096   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1097 
1098   if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
1099     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to revoke session key: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1100   }
1101 
1102   log_debug("Revoked key from session %s", session_string);
1103   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1104 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t database_session_start_migration
  (sqlite3 * db, const uint8_t session_id[16])


Start host migration (discovery mode). Called when the current
    host disconnects. Marks session as in_migration and starts a collection
    window for HOST_LOST candidates.




Parameters


db Database handle


session_id Session UUID


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 971 of file
  discovery/database.c.


971                                                                                               {
972   if (!db || !session_id) {
973     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db or session_id is NULL");
974   }
975 
976   uint64_t now_ns = time_get_realtime_ns();
977 
978   const char *sql = "UPDATE sessions SET "
979                     "in_migration = 1, "
980                     "migration_start_ns = ? "
981                     "WHERE session_id = ?";
982 
983   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
984   int rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
985   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
986     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare migration start: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
987   }
988 
989   sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 1, (sqlite3_int64)now_ns);
990   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 2, session_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
991 
992   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
993   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
994 
995   if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
996     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to start migration: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
997   }
998 
999   int changes = sqlite3_changes(db);
1000   if (changes == 0) {
1001     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Session not found for migration start");
1002   }
1003 
1004   log_info("Session migration started (session=%02x%02x..., start_ns=%" PRIu64 ")", session_id[0], session_id[1],
1005            now_ns);
1006 
1007   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1008 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, log_info,
    session_id, SET_ERRNO, and time_get_realtime_ns().






asciichat_error_t database_session_update_host (sqlite3 *
  db, const uint8_t session_id[16], const uint8_t
  host_participant_id[16], const char * host_address, uint16_t host_port,
  uint8_t connection_type)


Update session host (discovery mode). Called when a participant
    announces they are hosting. Updates the session's host fields so new joiners
    know where to connect.




Parameters


db Database handle


session_id Session UUID


host_participant_id Host's participant UUID


host_address Host's reachable address


host_port Host's port


connection_type How to reach host (ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_*)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 884 of file
  discovery/database.c.


886                                                                                             {
887   if (!db || !session_id || !host_participant_id) {
888     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db, session_id, or host_participant_id is NULL");
889   }
890 
891   uint64_t now = database_get_current_time_ms();
892 
893   const char *sql = "UPDATE sessions SET "
894                     "host_established = 1, "
895                     "host_participant_id = ?, "
896                     "host_address = ?, "
897                     "host_port = ?, "
898                     "host_connection_type = ?, "
899                     "last_activity_at = ? "
900                     "WHERE session_id = ?";
901 
902   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
903   int rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL);
904   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
905     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare host update: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
906   }
907 
908   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 1, host_participant_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
909   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 2, host_address ? host_address : "", -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
910   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 3, host_port);
911   sqlite3_bind_int(stmt, 4, connection_type);
912   sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 5, (sqlite3_int64)now);
913   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 6, session_id, 16, SQLITE_STATIC);
914 
915   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
916   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
917 
918   if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
919     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to update host: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
920   }
921 
922   int changes = sqlite3_changes(db);
923   if (changes == 0) {
924     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Session not found for host update");
925   }
926 
927   log_info("Session host updated: participant=%02x%02x..., address=%s:%u, type=%d", host_participant_id[0],
928            host_participant_id[1], host_address ? host_address : "(none)", host_port, connection_type);
929 
930   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
931 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, log_info,
    session_id, and SET_ERRNO.






bool database_session_verify_key (sqlite3 * db, const char
  * session_string, const uint8_t identity_pubkey[32])


Check if a key is valid for a session (exists and not
  revoked).


Parameters


db Database handle


session_string Session string


identity_pubkey Ed25519 public key to check (32 bytes)


Returns


true if key is valid, false otherwise


Definition at line 1106 of file
    discovery/database.c.


1106                                                                                                              {
1107   if (!db || !session_string || !identity_pubkey) {
1108     return false;
1109   }
1110 
1111   const char *sql = "SELECT 1 FROM session_keys WHERE session_string = ? AND identity_pubkey = ? AND revoked = 0";
1112 
1113   sqlite3_stmt *stmt = NULL;
1114   if (sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, sql, -1, &stmt, NULL) != SQLITE_OK) {
1115     return false;
1116   }
1117 
1118   sqlite3_bind_text(stmt, 1, session_string, -1, SQLITE_STATIC);
1119   sqlite3_bind_blob(stmt, 2, identity_pubkey, 32, SQLITE_STATIC);
1120 
1121   bool valid = (sqlite3_step(stmt) == SQLITE_ROW);
1122   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1123 
1124   return valid;
1125 }






References valid.






asciichat_error_t database_stats_session_count (sqlite3 *
  db, int64_t * count_out)


Get total number of active sessions.


Parameters


db Database handle


count_out Output: number of active sessions


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 1163 of file
    discovery/database.c.


1163                                                                                 {
1164   if (!db || !count_out) {
1165     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db or count_out is NULL");
1166   }
1167 
1168   const char *query = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sessions WHERE expires_at > ?";
1169   sqlite3_stmt *stmt;
1170   int rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, query, -1, &stmt, NULL);
1171   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
1172     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare statement: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1173   }
1174 
1175   time_t now = time(NULL);
1176   sqlite3_bind_int64(stmt, 1, (sqlite3_int64)now);
1177 
1178   int64_t count = 0;
1179   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
1180   if (rc == SQLITE_ROW) {
1181     count = sqlite3_column_int64(stmt, 0);
1182   } else if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
1183     sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1184     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to execute query: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1185   }
1186 
1187   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1188   *count_out = count;
1189 
1190   log_debug("Active session count: %lld", (long long)count);
1191   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1192 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug, and SET_ERRNO.






asciichat_error_t database_stats_unique_clients (sqlite3 *
  db, int64_t * count_out)


Get total number of unique clients/participants.


Parameters


db Database handle


count_out Output: number of unique participants


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code otherwise


Definition at line 1194 of file
    discovery/database.c.


1194                                                                                  {
1195   if (!db || !count_out) {
1196     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "db or count_out is NULL");
1197   }
1198 
1199   const char *query = "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT participant_id) FROM participants";
1200   sqlite3_stmt *stmt;
1201   int rc = sqlite3_prepare_v2(db, query, -1, &stmt, NULL);
1202   if (rc != SQLITE_OK) {
1203     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to prepare statement: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1204   }
1205 
1206   int64_t count = 0;
1207   rc = sqlite3_step(stmt);
1208   if (rc == SQLITE_ROW) {
1209     count = sqlite3_column_int64(stmt, 0);
1210   } else if (rc != SQLITE_DONE) {
1211     sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1212     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG, "Failed to execute query: %s", sqlite3_errmsg(db));
1213   }
1214 
1215   sqlite3_finalize(stmt);
1216   *count_out = count;
1217 
1218   log_debug("Unique clients count: %lld", (long long)count);
1219   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1220 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, ERROR_CONFIG,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, log_debug, and SET_ERRNO.
